subject.title: feat(google): support external URLs natively now that Gemini API accepts them
subject.body:
## Description

The Gemini API now natively supports external URLs (public HTTPS, signed URLs like S3 presigned, GCS URLs) passed directly via `fileUri` without needing to upload through the File API first.

**Announcement:** https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/gemini-api-new-file-limits/  
**Docs:** https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/file-input-methods

Valid Gemini API request:

```json
{
  "file_data": {
    "mime_type": "image/jpeg",
    "file_uri": "https://example.com/photo.jpg"
  }
}
```

However, `@ai-sdk/google` currently hardcodes `supportedUrls` to only pass through:
1. Google Files API URLs (`generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/files/*`)
2. YouTube URLs

Any other URL is downloaded by the core `ai` package and re-sent as base64 `inlineData`. This:
- Adds unnecessary latency (download + base64 encoding)
- Increases request payload size significantly
- Breaks for large files exceeding the 20MB inline limit
- Is no longer necessary since Gemini accepts external URLs directly

### Proposed change

Expand the `supportedUrls` configuration in the Google provider to include external HTTPS URLs so they are passed through as `fileUri` instead of being downloaded.

**Caveat from Google's docs:** External URL support is only available on **Gemini 2.5+ models** (not Gemini 2.0 family), so the implementation may need to be model-aware.

### Related issues

- #6558 — Previously `https://*` was used but removed because Gemini didn't support external URLs at the time. Now it does.
- #10692 — AI Gateway `fileUri` failures related to the same underlying `supportedUrls` limitation.
- #11402 — Gemini File API support PR (complementary — File API is for uploads, this is for direct URL passthrough).

### Use case

Applications with media already hosted on CDNs (CloudFront, Cloudflare) or using presigned URLs (S3, Azure Blob Storage) would benefit significantly — avoiding the round-trip download improves performance and reduces costs.

## AI SDK Version

- ai: 5.0.142
- @ai-sdk/google: 3.0.6

## Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct
